name: Diamond Head Beach
tagline: A hidden stretch below the lighthouse cliffs that locals use for bodyboarding, tidepooling, and escaping the Waikiki scene.
parking: Street parking along Diamond Head Rd near the lighthouse; walk down the cliffside trail
bestTime: South swell season (summer) for surf; morning for tidepools
talkStory: Hawaiians called Diamond Head 'Leahi' (brow of the tuna) for its profile. The steep cliffside scramble down keeps tourists away, making this a locals-only bodyboarding spot during south swells. Tide pools along the rocky shelf teem with sea urchins and small eels at low tide. The lighthouse above dates to 1899 and the surf break 'Lighthouse' just offshore is a serious wave that most visitors never see.
